questions.title: Name the biggest Park (area wise) in Pakistan?
questions.explanation:

questions.title: As per Article 33 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the State shall discourage _____ sectarian and provincial prejudices among the citizens.
questions.explanation:
Article 33 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 directs the State to discourage parochial, racial, tribal, sectarian, and provincial prejudices.

The aim is to promote unity and harmony among all citizens of Pakistan.

questions.title: In the case of Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), was filed against the dissolution of the National Assembly under Article _____ of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.
questions.explanation:
In PLD 1993 SC 473 (Nawaz Sharif vs. President of Pakistan), the case was filed against the dissolution of the National Assembly by the President under Article 58(2)(b).

This article (introduced by the 8th Amendment) empowered the President to dissolve the National Assembly, which was later repealed by the 13th Amendment.

questions.title: As per Article 207(3)(a) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a person who has held office as permanent judge of the Supreme Court _______ or act in any Court or before any authority in Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Article 207(3)(a) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 restricts a former Supreme Court judge from pleading or acting before any court or authority in Pakistan.

This ensures judicial independence and impartiality by avoiding conflicts of interest after retirement.

questions.title: As per Article 19-A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, every citizen shall have the right to have ______ in all matters of public importance subject to regulation and reasonable restrictions imposed by law.
questions.explanation:
Article 19-A grants citizens the right of access to information in all matters of public importance.

This right is subject to reasonable restrictions imposed by law for regulation and security purposes.

questions.title: As per Article 59(C) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Senate shall consist of how many general seats of Federal Capital?
questions.explanation:
Article 59(C) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 provides 2 general seats for the Federal Capital in the Senate.

These seats represent Islamabad directly, apart from the provincial representation.

questions.title: As per Article 203-C (2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Federal Shariat Court shall consist of not more than _____ Muslim Judges, including the Chief Justice, to be appointed by the President in accordance with Article 175A.
questions.explanation:
Article 203-C(2) (as amended) specifies that the Federal Shariat Court shall consist of not more than 8 Muslim Judges, including the Chief Justice.

These Judges are appointed by the President under Article 175A of the Constitution.

questions.title: As per Article 201 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, subject to Article 189, any decision of a _____ shall to the extent that it decides a question of law or is based upon or enunciates a principle of law, be binding on all courts subordinate to it.
questions.explanation:
Article 201 states that decisions of a High Court on questions or principles of law are binding on all subordinate courts.

This ensures consistency and uniformity in the application of law within a province.

questions.title: In the case of Khan Asfand Yar Wali and Others vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 S.C 607) _____ was the Presiding Officer/Chief Justice of Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Khan Asfand Yar Wali vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 SC 607) was decided when Irshad Hassan Khan was the Presiding Officer and Chief Justice of Pakistan.

This case is often cited regarding constitutional interpretation and federal powers in Pakistan.

questions.title: As per Article 1(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, Pakistan shall be ______ Republic to be known as the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Article 1(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 states that “Pakistan shall be a Federal Republic to be known as the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.”

This highlights Pakistan’s system of government as a federation of provinces under one republic.

questions.title: As per Article 63-A(6) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the defection clauses shall not apply to the _____?
questions.answer: Chairman or Speaker of a House
questions.explanation:
Article 63-A(6) states that the defection clauses do not apply to the Chairman or Speaker of a House.

This exemption exists because they are expected to act impartially in conducting proceedings.

questions.title: Article 12 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, deals with the protection against _____?
questions.explanation:
Article 12 of the Constitution of Pakistan prohibits laws that impose retrospective punishment for an act committed before it was made an offence.

This ensures fairness by protecting individuals from being punished under ex-post facto laws.

questions.title: As per the Case of Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), the precise question the Supreme Court was whether the High Court had jurisdiction under Article 98 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1962 to enquire into the validity of detention under the Martial Law Regulation No. _____ of 1971.
questions.explanation:
In Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 SC 139), the key issue was whether the High Court had jurisdiction under Article 98 of the 1962 Constitution to question detention under Martial Law Regulation No. 78 of 1971.

The Supreme Court held that Martial Law was unconstitutional, restoring the jurisdiction of High Courts in such matters.

questions.title: As per the case Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C 240), the President of the Constituent Assembly Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan filed a writ petition in the nature of _____ with a view to determining the validity of appellants appointments as members of the Council of Minister.
questions.explanation:
Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan filed the writ petition in the nature of Mandamus and Quo Warranto.
The petition sought to compel performance of duty and question the authority of office holders.

questions.title: As per Article 204(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, as is regarding contempt of Court, the Court means the _____?
questions.answer: Supreme Court or a High Court
questions.explanation:
Article 204(1) of the Constitution specifies that for contempt jurisdiction, Court means the Supreme Court or a High Court.

This limits contempt powers to higher judiciary, excluding lower courts.

questions.title: As per Article 65 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a person elected to a House shall not sit or vote until he has made before the House oath in the form set out in ______ the schedule.
questions.explanation:
According to Article 65 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a person elected to a House cannot sit or vote until they fulfill a formal requirement.

They must take an oath before the House in the form set out in the Third Schedule of the Constitution.

questions.title: As per Article 70(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, a Bill with respect to any matter in the ______ may originate in either House and shall, if it is passed by the House in which it originated, be transmitted to the other House; and if the Bill is passed without amendment by the other House also, it shall be presented to the President for assent.
questions.explanation:
Article 70(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 provides that a Bill relating to matters in the Federal Legislative List can originate in either House (National Assembly or Senate).

Once passed by one House and agreed without amendment by the other, it is sent to the President for assent.

questions.title: As per the Case of Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), filed against the detention of Malik Ghulam Jillani which was dismissed by the Lahore High Court, one of the questions before the Supreme Court was whether the doctrine enunciated in the case of _____ was correct.
questions.explanation:
In Miss Asma Jillani vs Government of Pakistan (PLD 1972 S.C 139), the Court examined the validity of the doctrine of necessity.

This doctrine had been enunciated in State vs Dosso (PLD 1958 SC 533), which the Court reviewed and overruled.

questions.title: As per Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the term of five years shall commence from the day of ______ and shall stand dissolved at the expiration of its term.
questions.explanation:
The 5-year term of the National Assembly starts from the day of its first meeting.

It automatically dissolves at the end of the term, unless dissolved earlier.

questions.title: As per Article 198(4) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, each of the High Courts may have Benches at such other places as the Governor may determine on the advice of the _____ and in consultation with the Chief Justice of the High Court.
questions.explanation:
Article 198(4) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 states:

"Each High Court may have Benches at such other places as the Governor may determine on the advice of the Cabinet and in consultation with the Chief Justice of the High Court."

questions.title: As per case "Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1996 S.C. 324"), " consultation " is defined as effective, ______, purposive, consensus oriented, leaving no room for complaint of arbitrariness or unfair play.
questions.explanation:
In Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1996 SC 324), the Supreme Court held that consultation must be effective and meaningful, not a mere formality.

The Court emphasized that it should be purposive and consensus-oriented, preventing arbitrariness or unfair play.

questions.title: As per Article 32 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the State shall encourage local Government institutions composed of ______ representative of the areas concerned and in such institutions, special representation will be given to peasants, workers and women.
questions.explanation:
Article 32 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 directs the State to encourage local government institutions composed of elected representatives of the concerned areas.

It also ensures special representation for peasants, workers, and women in these institutions.

questions.title: As per Article 183(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the permanent seat of the Supreme Court shall, subjects to clause, be at ______?

questions.title: As per Article 52 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, whenever it is necessary to cause a woman to be searched, the search shall be made by another woman, with strict regard to _____?
questions.explanation:
Article 52 ensures the dignity and modesty of women during any search procedure.

It requires that the search be conducted by another woman with strict regard to decency.

questions.title: As per Article 4(2) of the United Nations Charter, the admission of any such State to membership in the United Nations will be effected by a decision of the ______ upon the recommendation of the Security Council.
questions.explanation:
• New members are admitted to the UN through a formal process.
  • The Security Council first recommends the applicant state.
  • The final decision is made by the General Assembly.
  • Both organs must agree for admission.
  • This ensures collective approval of new UN membership.


Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

questions.title: As per Article 193(2) of Constitution Pakistan 1973, a person shall not be appointed a Judge of a High Court unless he is a citizen of Pakistan, is not less than forty- years of age and he has, for a period of not less than ____ years, held a judicial office in Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Article 193(2) sets the eligibility criteria for High Court judges, including 10 years of holding a judicial office in Pakistan (or other qualifying legal experience).

This ensures sufficient professional and judicial experience before elevation to the High Court.

questions.title: As per Article 185(2)(c) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, an appeal shall lie to the Supreme Court from any judgment, decree, final order or sentence of a High Court if the High Court has imposed any punishment on any person for _____?
questions.explanation:
Article 185(2)(c) grants the right to appeal to the Supreme Court in cases where the High Court imposes punishment for contempt of court.

This ensures judicial oversight and a safeguard against misuse of contempt powers.

questions.title: As per case Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C 240), the President of Constituent Assembly ______ filed a writ petition in the nature of mandamus and quo warranto.
questions.explanation:
Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan, as President of the Constituent Assembly, challenged the Governor General’s dissolution of the Assembly.

He sought writs of mandamus and quo warranto from the Federal Court.

questions.title: As per the case of Zafar Ali Shah and others vs General Pervez Musharraf, Chief Executive of Pakistan (PLD 2000 S.C 869), filed under ______ of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.
questions.explanation:
Article 184(3) gives the Supreme Court original jurisdiction in matters of public importance relating to enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

The Zafar Ali Shah case was filed directly in the Supreme Court under this provision.

questions.title: As per Article 2A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the principles and provisions set out in the objective resolution reproduced in the Annex are here by made _____ part of the Constitution and shall have effect accordingly.
questions.explanation:
Article 2A declares the Objective Resolution as a substantive part of the Constitution.

This means it has binding legal effect in constitutional interpretation and application.

questions.title: As per Article 14(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, no person shall be subjected to _____ for the purpose of extracting evidence.
questions.explanation:
Article 14(2) explicitly prohibits torture for obtaining evidence.

This provision safeguards human dignity and personal rights.

questions.title: As per Article 23 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, every citizen shall have right to acquire, hold and dispose of property in any part of Pakistan, subject to the _____ any reasonable restrictions the imposed by law in the public interest.
questions.explanation:
Article 23 grants property rights but allows restrictions subject to the Constitution.

Such restrictions must be reasonable and in the public interest.

questions.title: As per Article 55(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, subject to the Constitution, all decisions of the National Assembly shall be taken by _____ of the members present and voting, but the person presiding shall not vote except in the case of equality of votes.
questions.explanation:
Article 55(1) states that decisions in the National Assembly are made by a majority of members present and voting.

The presiding officer only votes in case of a tie.

questions.title: As per Article 77 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, no tax shall be levied for the purposes of the _____ except by or under the authority of Act of Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament).
questions.explanation:
• No tax can be imposed without legal authority.
  • Taxes must be approved by Parliament.
  • It applies to the Federation.
  • Ensures a lawful taxation system.
  • Protects citizens from arbitrary taxation.


Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

questions.title: As per Article 13(1) of Statute of the International Court of Justice, the members of the Court shall be elected for ______ years and may be re-elected.
questions.explanation:
• The provision is stated in Article 13(1) of the ICJ Statute.
  • Judges of the International Court of Justice are elected members.
  • The term of office is nine (9) years.


Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

questions.title: As per Article 68 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, No discussion shall take place in Majlis-e-Shoora (Parliament) with respect to the conduct of any _____ in the discharge of his duties.
questions.answer: Judge of the Supreme Court or of a High Court
questions.explanation:
Article 68 Constitution 1973 prohibits parliamentary discussion on the conduct of judges of the Supreme Court or High Courts regarding their official duties.

This provision safeguards judicial independence from legislative interference.

questions.title: As per the Judgment of the case of Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C 240), the application was heard by a Full Bench of ______ Judges of whom Mr. Justice Hassan Ali Agha retired during the hearing and therefore gave no opinion.
questions.explanation:
According to the judgment in Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs. Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 FC 240), the case was heard by a Full Bench of five judges.

Justice Hassan Ali Agha retired during the hearing and did not contribute to the final decision.

questions.title: The boundary line between India and Pakistan demarcated by Sir Cyril Radcliffe is called:
questions.explanation:

In order to determine exactly which territories to assign to each country, in June 1947,

Britain appointed Sir Cyril Radcliffe to chair two boundary commissions

questions.title: As per Article 205 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the remuneration and other terms and conditions of service of a Judge of the Supreme Court or of a High Court shall be as provided in the ____ Schedule.
questions.explanation:
Article 205 of the Constitution 1973 specifies that the remuneration and service terms of Supreme Court and High Court judges are provided in the Fifth Schedule.

This ensures judicial independence and standardized conditions across Pakistan.

questions.title: As per Article 189 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, any decision of the Supreme Court shall, to the extent that it decides a ______ or is based upon or enunciates a principle of law, be binding on all other courts in Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Article 189 makes Supreme Court decisions binding on all other courts in Pakistan when they decide a question of law or lay down a legal principle.

This ensures uniformity and consistency in the interpretation of laws across the country.

questions.title: As per Article 178 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, before entering upon office, the Chief Justice of Pakistan shall make before the ______ and any other Judge of the Supreme Court shall make before the Chief Justice, oath in the form set out in the Third Schedule.
questions.explanation:
According to Article 178 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, before entering office, the Chief Justice of Pakistan shall make an oath before the President, and any other Judge of the Supreme Court shall make an oath before the Chief Justice.

The form of the oath is outlined in the Third Schedule of the Constitution.

questions.title: As per Article 2A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the principles and provisions set out in the ______ reproduced in the Annex are here by made substantive part of the Constitution and shall have effect accordingly.
questions.explanation:
Article 2A makes the Objectives Resolution a substantive part of the Constitution.

Its principles and provisions are given full effect as part of the constitutional framework.

questions.title: As per Article 56(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the ______ may address either House or both Houses assembled together and may for that purpose require the attendance of the members.
questions.explanation:
Article 56(1) allows the President to address either House or both Houses of Parliament.

The President can require the attendance of members for this purpose.

questions.title: As per Article 190 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, all executive and judicial authorities throughout Pakistan shall act in aid of the ______?
questions.explanation:
Article 190 requires that all executive and judicial authorities act in aid of the Supreme Court.

This ensures the Supreme Court’s directives and orders are effectively implemented across Pakistan.

questions.title: As per Article 35 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the ______ shall protect the marriage, the family, the mother and the child.
questions.explanation:
Article 35 mandates that the State is responsible for protecting marriage, family, mother, and child.

It ensures legal and social safeguards for family and maternal welfare.

questions.title: As per the case of Zafar Ali Shah and others vs General Pervez Musharraf, Chief Executive of Pakistan (PLD 2000 S.C 869), Pervez Musharraf took ______ steps and proclaimed emergency as Chief Executive.
questions.explanation:
In this case, Pervez Musharraf took extra-constitutional steps to assume power.

He proclaimed emergency and acted as Chief Executive outside the framework of the Constitution.

questions.title: As per Article 53(8) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, when the National Assembly is dissolved, the ______ shall continue in his office till the person elected to fill the office by the next Assembly enters upon his office.
questions.explanation:
• The provision is given in Article 53(8) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.
  • It deals with the office of the Speaker of the National Assembly.
  • If the National Assembly is dissolved, the Speaker continues in office.
  • The Speaker remains until the next elected Assembly selects a new Speaker.

Last updated by NS on 17-April-2026

questions.title: As per Article 87(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, each House shall have ______?
questions.explanation:
Article 87(1) provides that each House of Parliament shall have its own Secretariat.

The Secretariat manages administrative and procedural functions of the respective House.

questions.title: As per Case of Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff (PLD 1977 S.C. 657), _____ the learned Attorney General for Pakistan, appearing as the law officer of the court.
questions.explanation:
Mr. Sharifuddin Pirzada appeared as the Attorney General for Pakistan in this case.

He acted in the capacity of the law officer of the Court during the proceedings.

questions.title: As per Article 25(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, there shall be no discrimination on the basis of ______?
questions.explanation:
According to Article 25(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, there shall be no discrimination on the basis of sex.
Article 25 also states that all citizens are equal before the law and entitled to equal protection of the law.

questions.title: As per Article 194 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, before entering upon office, the Chief Justice of a High Court shall make before the _____ and any other Judge of the Court shall make before the Chief Justice, oath in the form set out in the Third Schedule.
questions.explanation:
Article 194 states the Chief Justice of a High Court takes oath before the Governor of the province.
Other High Court judges take oath before the Chief Justice of that court.

questions.title: As per Judgment of the case "Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff and Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1977 S.C 657)", the petition seeks to challenge the detention of Mr. Zulifqar Ali Bhutto, and _____ other leaders of the Pakistan People's Party under Marital Law Order No. 12 of 1977.
questions.explanation:
In Begum Nusrat Bhutto vs Chief of Army Staff, the petition challenged the detention of Zulfikar Ali Bhutto and 10 other PPP leaders.

The detention was under Martial Law Order No. 12 of 1977 after the military takeover.

questions.title: As per case "Khan Asfand Yar Wali and Others vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 2001 S.C 607)", the writ petition was filed under _____ of the Constitution of Pakistan, wherein vires of National Accountability Bureau Ordinance, 1999, was challenged.
questions.explanation:
The petition in Khan Asfand Yar Wali vs Federation was filed under Article 184(3) directly in the Supreme Court.

It challenged the constitutionality of the NAB Ordinance, 1999, on grounds of public importance and fundamental rights.

questions.title: As per Article 53(6) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Deputy Speaker may, by writing under his hand addressed to the _____, resign his office.
questions.explanation:
Article 53(6) states the Deputy Speaker can resign by writing to the Speaker.

This maintains procedural hierarchy within the National Assembly.

questions.title: As per Article 22(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, in respect of any ______ institution, there shall be no discrimination against any community in the granting of exemption or concession in relation to taxation.
questions.explanation:
• It is mentioned in Article 22(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973.
  • It relates to taxation exemptions or concessions.
  • It ensures no discrimination among communities.
  • The protection applies specifically to religious institutions.


Last updated by NS on 16-April-2026

questions.title: As per Article 57 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Prime Minister, a Federal Minister, a Minister of State and the _____ General shall have the right to speak and otherwise take part in the proceedings of either House, or a joint sitting or any committee thereof.
questions.explanation:
Article 57 grants the Prime Minister, ministers, and the Attorney General the right to speak in Parliament or its committees.

This right exists even if they are not members of that particular House.

questions.title: As per Article 13(b) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, no person _____, when accused of an offence, be compelled to be a witness against himself.
questions.explanation:
Article 13(b) states that no person shall be compelled to be a witness against themselves.

This protects individuals from self-incrimination in legal proceedings.

questions.title: As per Islamic Jurisprudence by Imran Ahsan Khan Niazi, the Fiqh is used in the literal sense means " ______ " and "discernment".
questions.explanation:
In its literal sense, Fiqh means "understanding" and "discernment" in Islamic jurisprudence.

It refers to deep comprehension of religious rulings and principles.

questions.title: As per Article 37(g) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the ____ shall prevent prostitution, gambling and taking of injurious drugs, printing, publication, circulation and display of obscene literature and advertisements.
questions.explanation:
Article 37(g) mandates the State to prevent social evils like prostitution, gambling, and harmful drug use.

It also includes stopping the circulation and display of obscene literature and advertisements to protect public morality.

questions.title: As per Article 78(2)(a) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, all other moneys received by or on behalf of the _____; shall be credited to the Public Account of the Federation.
questions.explanation:
Article 78(2)(a) mandates that all moneys received by or on behalf of the Federal Government be credited to the Public Account of the Federation.

This ensures centralized management of public funds at the federal level.

questions.title: As per Article 10(8) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the appreciate _____ shall determine the place of detention of the person detained and fix a reasonable subsistence allowance for his family.
questions.explanation:
Article 10(8) of the Constitution assigns the Review Board the duty to decide the detention place and subsistence allowance.

This protects detainees’ rights and supports their families during detention.

questions.title: As per case "Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473)", President ______ dismissed Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif with his cabinet.
questions.explanation:
In Muhammad Nawaz Sharif vs President of Pakistan (PLD 1993 S.C 473), Ghulam Ishaq Khan, then President, dismissed Nawaz Sharif’s government.

This was under the controversial use of constitutional powers at that time.

questions.title: As per case "Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan (PLD 1996 S.C. 324"), " ______ " is defined as effective, meaningful, purposive, consensus oriented, leaving no room for complaint of arbitrariness or unfair play.
questions.explanation:
In Al-Jehad Trust vs Federation of Pakistan, consultation is defined as a process that is effective, meaningful, and consensus-oriented.

It emphasizes fairness and absence of arbitrariness in decision-making.

questions.title: As per Article 193(2) of Constitution Pakistan 1973, A person shall not be appointed a Judge of a High Court unless he is a citizen of Pakistan, is not less than ______ years of age.
questions.explanation:
Article 193(2) of the 1973 Constitution requires a High Court judge to be at least 45 years old.

This ensures adequate legal experience and maturity before appointment.

questions.title: As per case The State vs Dosso and other (PLD 1958 S.C. 533), by the Proclamation of October 7, 1958, the President of Pakistan annulled the Constitution of 2nd March and dissolved the ______?
questions.explanation:
In The State vs Dosso (PLD 1958 SC 533), the Court validated the annulment of the 1956 Constitution and dissolution of National and Provincial Assemblies.

This case applied the "Doctrine of Necessity" for the first time in Pakistan.

questions.title: In the case Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 F.C. 240, ______ was the Chief Justice.
questions.explanation:
In Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Khan vs Governor General of Pakistan (PLD 1955 FC 240), Justice Muhammad Munir was the Chief Justice.

This case is notable for upholding the Governor General’s powers, leading to controversy over constitutional interpretation.

questions.title: As per Article 198(3) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Lahore High Court Shall have a Bench each at Bahawalpur, Multan and Rawalpindi, the High Court of Sindh shall have a Bench at ______?
questions.explanation:
Article 198(3) of the 1973 Constitution provides that the Sindh High Court shall have a Bench at Sukkur.

This facilitates easier access to justice for residents of upper Sindh.

questions.title: As per Article 53(4) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Speaker or the Deputy Speaker shall NOT preside at a meeting of the Assembly when a/an ______ for his removal from office is being considered.
questions.explanation:
Article 53(4) states that the Speaker or Deputy Speaker cannot preside over an Assembly meeting when a resolution for their removal is under consideration.

This prevents any conflict of interest during such proceedings.

questions.title: As per Article 5(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, obedience to the ______ is the inviolable obligation of every citizen wherever he may be and of every other person for the time being within Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Article 5(2) of the 1973 Constitution makes obedience to the Constitution and the law an inviolable obligation for all citizens and persons in Pakistan.

This ensures the supremacy of the Constitution and rule of law.

questions.title: As per Provision of Article 54(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the National Assembly shall meet for NOT less than _____ working days in each year.
questions.explanation:
Article 54(2) of the 1973 Constitution mandates that the National Assembly must meet for at least 130 working days each year.

This ensures regular legislative activity and parliamentary oversight.

questions.title: As per Article 36 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the State shall safeguard the ______ rights and interests of minorities, including their due representation in the Federal and Provincial services.
questions.explanation:
• Article 36 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973, deals with the protection of minorities.
  • It states that the State shall safeguard the legitimate rights and interests of minorities.
  • This includes ensuring their due representation in Federal and Provincial services.

Last updated on NS-16-April-2026

questions.title: As per provision of Article 194 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, the Chief Justice of the Islamabad High Court shall make oath before the ______ and other Judges of that Court shall make oath before the Chief Justice of the Islamabad High Court.
questions.explanation:
Article 194 states that the Chief Justice of the Islamabad High Court takes the oath before the President of Pakistan.

Other judges of the Islamabad High Court take their oath before its Chief Justice.

questions.title: As per Article 11(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, all forms of forced labor and traffic in human beings are _____?
questions.explanation:
Article 11(2) of the 1973 Constitution explicitly states that all forms of forced labor and human trafficking are prohibited.

This provision safeguards human dignity and fundamental rights.

questions.title: As per Article 1(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, Pakistan shall be Federal Republic to be known as the _______ Republic of Pakistan.
questions.explanation:
Article 1(1) of the 1973 Constitution declares Pakistan as the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.

This reflects the country’s identity as both a federal state and an Islamic nation.

questions.explanation:
The Two-Nation Theory states that Muslims and Hindus of the Indian subcontinent are two distinct nations, with their own religion, culture, and identity.
It was the ideological foundation for the creation of Pakistan in 1947.

questions.title: Which of the following countries has not been recognized by Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
• Pakistan does not recognize Armenia due to its conflict with Azerbaijan over Nagorno-Karabakh.
  • Pakistan is the only country that has not established diplomatic relations with Armenia.

questions.title: The following country does not share its border with Pakistan? Iran,India, Uzbekistan, Afghanistan
questions.explanation:
Pakistan shares borders with India, Afghanistan, Iran, and China, but not with Uzbekistan.

Uzbekistan is separated from Pakistan by Afghanistan’s Wakhan Corridor.

questions.title: Who among the following has become the second Army Chief to be promoted to the rank of Field Marshal in the history of Pakistan Army?
questions.explanation:
On 20 May 2025, the federal cabinet approved the promotion of Chief of Army Staff (COAS) General Asim Munir to Field Marshal.

For ensuring the security of the country and defeating the enemy based on the high strategy and courageous leadership of the Battle of Truth, Operation Bunyan-um-Marsoos.

questions.title: When Muhammad Ali Jinnah gave 14 points?
questions.explanation:
• In March 1929, Muhammad Ali Jinnah presented his 14 Points during a Muslim League session in Delhi to protect Muslim rights and interests.
    • He had left for England in May 1928.
    • He had returned after six months.
    • The Nehru Report of 1928 influenced these points, which were consolidated with help from Muhammad Ali Jauhar.
    • Jinnah’s Fourteen Points (1929) aimed to safeguard Muslim rights in British India.

    They included:

    1. Federal constitution with provincial powers.
    2. Provincial autonomy.
    3. No constitutional changes without state agreement.
    4. Adequate Muslim representation in legislatures.
    5. Muslim representation in services and self-governing bodies.
    6. 1/3rd Muslim representation in the Central Legislature.
    7. 1/3rd Muslim members in central and state cabinets.
    8. Separate electorates.
    9. No bill passed if 3/4th of a minority opposes it.
    10. Reorganisation should not affect Muslim majorities in Bengal, Punjab, and NWFP.
    11. Separation of Sindh from Bombay Presidency.
    12. Reforms in NWFP and Balochistan.
    13. Full religious freedom.
    14. Protection of Muslim religious, cultural, educational, and linguistic rights.

questions.title: What was the original name of Hazrat Lal Shahbaz Qalandar?
questions.explanation:
The full name of Hazrat Lal Shahbaz Qalandar: Syed Shah Hussain Marwandi

Lal Shahbaz Qalandar wrote several books in Persian and Arabic

Lal Shahbaz Qalandar was a Sufi saint and poet of present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan

Born: 1177, Marand, Iran

Died: February 19, 1274, Sehwān

Place of burial: Darbar Hazrat Usman Marvandi Lal Shahbaz Qalandar, Sehwān

Title: Qalandar

The shrine of Hazrat Laal Shahbaz Qalandar is situated in Sehwan Sindh

questions.title: According to the World Bank, the population of Pakistan in 2020 was _______?
questions.explanation:
Based on various sources, including Statista, Dawn, and the UN, the population of Pakistan in 2020 was 220 million. 

The current population of Pakistan is 255,500,337 as of Wednesday, July 30, 2025, based on Worldometer's elaboration of the latest United Nations data.

questions.title: The Largest Island of Pakistan is ________?
questions.explanation:
Astola is Pakistan's largest offshore island at approximately 6.7 km (4.2 mi) long)

It is also known as Jezira Haft Talar, Satadip, or 'Island of the Seven Hills

Location:  Balochistan, Gwadar District

Astola is famous for its rich marine biodiversity.

questions.title: Which constitution named Pakistan as Islamic Republic of Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
After nine years of efforts, Pakistan was successful in framing a constitution.

The Constituent Assembly adopted it on 29 February 1956

it was enforced on 23 March 1956 proclaiming Pakistan to be an Islamic republic.

This constituent consists of:

234 Articles divided into 13 parts and 6 schedules.


23rd March became our Republic day

In 1957 Pakistan officially renamed the Islamic Republic of Pakistan
